_notes: X2-U1 WORLD-grounded fixture (real, source != authored). Block P (predictable recurrence): the DEP0033 status per the ORIGINAL FILING -> answer 'deprecated' (record dep0033-deprecated, offered every P episode). dep0033-revoked is not offered at top_k=1, so C prunes it; it is the round-trip record re-needed in block U. The two status records are parallel so only the discriminator ('original filing' vs 'current ruling') decides retrieval, at any recency; questions never contain the answer words. Identical record universe across the sequence. Out-of-weights earned by obscurity+recency, proven by the manifest ignorance probe. World oracle: web_verified.
question: Per the DEP0033 original filing, what is the status of the module-level events.listenerCount()? Answer in one word.

oracle_ref.representativeness: Node DEP0033 status as originally filed (v3.2.0); real version-controlled fact, source != authored.

records.text: Unrelated Node deprecations archive: assorted DEP0XXX notices covering legacy Buffer constructors, the domain module, _linklist, several crypto cipher defaults, the punycode bundle, url query handling, util.isArray and related type-check helpers, and documentation-only migration notices consolidated across the v4 through v12 release lines.
DEP0033 original filing, Node v3.2.0: the module-level events.listenerCount() is deprecated.
DEP0033 current ruling, Node v24.14.0: the module-level events.listenerCount() is revoked.
